questions.question: Define and differentiate between monolithic and microkernel OS structures. Give one example of each architecture.
keyPoints: Monolithic: all OS services in kernel space, single large executable (e.g., Unix, Linux)
Microkernel: minimal kernel with services in user space (e.g., MINIX, QNX)
Monolithic offers better performance but less modularity
Microkernel provides better reliability, security, and extensibility

questions.question: Explain the boot process of an operating system from power-on to the loading of the OS kernel. List at least four major steps involved.
keyPoints: Boot loader locates and loads OS kernel from storage device
Kernel initializes core system resources (memory, CPU, drivers)
System services and user-space programs are started (init/systemd)

questions.question: Describe the layered and hybrid OS structures in detail. Compare their advantages and disadvantages, and explain why modern operating systems like Windows and macOS adopt hybrid architecture.
keyPoints: Layered: OS divided into hierarchical layers (hardware → kernel → system calls → user interface)
Hybrid: combines monolithic and microkernel approaches (e.g., Windows NT, macOS XNU)
Advantages: layered provides simplicity and abstraction; hybrid offers performance + modularity
Modern OS prefer hybrid for balancing security, stability, and performance

questions.question: Explain the role of system programs in an operating system. Differentiate between compilers, interpreters, and assemblers. Additionally, describe how memory management programs (like memory allocators and virtual memory managers) contribute to system efficiency.
keyPoints: System programs provide environment for application execution (file management, process control, device drivers)
Compilers translate entire source code to machine code; interpreters execute line-by-line; assemblers convert assembly to machine code
Memory management programs handle allocation/deallocation, paging, and virtual memory translation
Efficient memory management maximizes utilization, provides isolation, and enables multitasking
